Teacher controls

Generate Luka codes to give to students, post announcements, and view class data. To exit teacher mode, remove ?teacher from the URL.

🪙 Generate Luka code

Codes work on any device — give them to students via Post-it, text, Google Classroom, etc.

📢 Post announcement

Note: announcements are saved on this device only. To share with all students, copy the JSON below and paste into the ANNOUNCEMENTS array in the HTML.

📋 Export announcements as JSON

Copy this and paste into the file's ANNOUNCEMENTS array, then re-share the file.

✍ Post writing or speaking topic

Topics saved on this device only. Use the export below to share with all students.

📋 Export topics as JSON

Copy and paste these into the WRITING_TOPICS or SPEAKING_TOPICS array, then re-share the file with students.

📊 Class data on this device

Shows progress for every student profile created on this browser. Useful when sharing a classroom iPad.

Pick your avatar

Choose an emoji or upload an image. Saved to your device only.

Spanish Class · Curso 2025–2026

Bienvenido
al Pueblo

A space to practice, listen, and share. Built by students, for students — where every word tells a story and every mistake is a beginning. El pueblo means "the village" — a place where everyone knows your name and learns together.

01 / Words Vocabulary Flashcards · 5 themed decks 02 / Grammar Verb practice 5 tenses · regular & irregular 03 / Text Guided reading Passages with vocab & quiz 04 / Sound Listening Audio at different levels 05 / Pen Writing topics Respond in writing to teacher prompts 06 / Voice Speaking topics Record responses to teacher prompts 07 / World Geography & culture 21 countries · flags · traditions 📘 Lessons Grammar lessons Articles · verbs · ser/estar · agreement 🎮 Games Practice games Numbers · dates · days of the week ★ Rewards Lukas & achievements Your progress and coins 🛍 Shop Spend your Lukas Digital unlocks & teacher rewards

dawn / early hours

Learn without pressure.

Each card is a small window into the language — pronunciation, context, and an example phrase to help you remember. Tap the card to see the translation.

Verb drill

02 / 07

Twenty random verbs. Fill in every blank, then hit Submit drill for instant feedback and your score. Earn Lukas based on accuracy — but you only get one shot per drill.

Choose your tense:
Verb group:
Include vosotros form:

Grammar lessons

★ / Lecciones

Foundational Spanish grammar, broken into bite-sized lessons. Each lesson has a short explanation, examples, and a quick check at the end. Read at your own pace — they don't disappear.

Level
← Back to lessons

Practice games

★ / Juegos

Quick games for the basics — days, dates, and numbers. Each game is 10 rounds. Score-based Lukas: the better you do, the more you earn.

🃏

Match it

Pick any vocab deck and race the clock to match Spanish to English. New every time.

Any of 25+ decks · beat your best time
📅

Days of the week

Lunes, martes, miércoles… can you name them all?

10 rounds · up to 25 Lukas
🗓️

Speaking dates

How do you say "March 15" in Spanish? Practice writing dates.

10 rounds · up to 35 Lukas
🔢

Numbers 1–30

Uno, dos, tres… start small. Master the foundations.

10 rounds · up to 25 Lukas
🔠

Numbers 30–100

Treinta y cinco… learn the "y" pattern and the trickier tens.

10 rounds · up to 35 Lukas
💯

Numbers 100–9999

Trescientos cuarenta y siete… big numbers take big concentration.

10 rounds · up to 50 Lukas
🕐

Telling time

¿Qué hora es? "Son las tres y media" — practice every form.

10 rounds · up to 40 Lukas
🌤️

The weather

Hace sol, llueve, está nublado — describe what you see.

10 rounds · up to 30 Lukas
🗣️

Tú vs. Usted

Pick the right formality for each social situation.

10 rounds · up to 30 Lukas
✍️

Adjective agreement

Type the right form, or fix the wrong one. Gender & number agreement.

10 rounds · up to 35 Lukas
🚿

Daily routine reflexives

Me levanto, te duchas, se acuestan — conjugate with the right pronoun.

10 rounds · up to 35 Lukas
🎯

Object pronouns

Lo, la, le, les — pick the right pronoun to replace each noun.

10 rounds · up to 35 Lukas

Reading library

03 / 07

Stories and poems for every level — from beginner sketches to classic verse by Lorca, Machado, and Martí. Earn Lukas based on difficulty: harder texts pay more.

← Back to library

Listening practice

04 / 07

Train your ear with three kinds of exercises: dictation, comprehension, and fill-in-the-blank.

Note: Audio uses your device's built-in Spanish voice. It can sound robotic — that's normal. Use the speed controls to slow it down when needed.
Speed

Writing topics

05 / 07

Pick a topic and write your response in Spanish. Your work is saved to your device — you can come back to it later. Long responses (200+ words) earn extra Lukas.

Level
Type

Speaking topics

06 / 07

Pick a topic and record your response in Spanish. Speak naturally — the goal is fluency, not perfection. Recordings 30+ seconds long earn more Lukas.

New to speaking? Start with the Read-aloud topics — they give you a script so you can focus only on pronunciation. Then move up to Guided, then Open.
Level
Type
← Back to topics

The Spanish-speaking world

07 / 07

Twenty-one countries, hundreds of millions of speakers. Each with its own flavor, rhythm, and unique words. Tap a flag to discover its capital, typical food, and important cultural figures.

Known for…

★ Cultural identity

A quick snapshot of what each country is famous for around the world — its food, music, traditions, and exports. These are the things you'd hear about if you mentioned the country to someone who'd never been.

People to know

★ Internationally famous figures

Writers, artists, athletes, musicians, and leaders from across the Spanish-speaking world. Click any person to learn more.

Slang & expressions

★ The untranslatables

Spanish isn't one language — it's twenty-one of them. These are phrases native speakers use every day that you won't find in any textbook. Most translate weirdly into English (or not at all). Tap a card to flip and see what it really means and where it's used.

Iconic dishes

★ What to actually eat

The Spanish-speaking world is a food paradise — and most students have only heard the names. Tap a card to learn what each dish actually is, where it comes from, and why people love it.

Mini game · 2 Lukas per correct flag

Guess the flag.

Which country does this flag belong to? Get it right to earn Lukas. Streaks of 5 give a bonus.

Correct: 0 · Attempts: 0

Identify the place.

The shop

★ / Tienda

Spend your Lukas. Some rewards unlock new things on the site instantly. Others — like a homework pass or class snack — generate a redemption code you'll show your teacher.
